rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL PEREZ et al VS. MARIO DUARTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 9, 2012, Line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT ARTURO ROVERSO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first, second and fifth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Cross-Complainants adequately pled the first, second and fifth causes of actions. For statute of limitations purposes, the cross-complaint relates back to when the complaint was filed. The interpleader cross-claim is not cognizable because cross-complainants allege ownership of the property. rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Deeming Req.'S For Admission Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, January 9, 2012, line 2, DEFENDANT HAMILTON, RICCI & ASSOCIATES, INCORPORATED'S Motion For An Order Deeming Req.'S For Admission Admitted And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Pro Tem Judge Renne has decided not to issue a tentative ruling. Each party should bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS T. MANGANO VS. THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Mervyn Maze, The Regents Of The University Of California'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 9, 2012, Line 16. Defendants Mervyn Maze and The Regents Of The University Of California'S Demurrer To Complaint. Sustained as to all four causes of action without leave to amend as to Mervyn Maze and sustained without leave to amend as to the first and second cause of actions and with ten days leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action as to the Regents. As to Mr. Maze, none of the causes of action lie since it is the Regents, if anyone, who has or can take action with regard to plaintiff's position. Plaintiff is only seeking damages under the first cause of action, which is not available. (Katzberg v. UC Regents (2002) 29 Cal.4th 300; Lu v. Hawaiian Gardens Casino, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 592, 602). As to the second cause of action, plaintiff's remedy is to pursue a mandamus claim. (CCP 1061). As to the third cause of action, plaintiff cannot pursue the common law wrongful termination cause of action but has leave to plead a claim with a statutory basis. As to the fourth cause of action , plaintiff is granted leave to plead a traditional mandate claim per CCP 1085. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HYLEN VS. WILLIAM MITCHELL RYDER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 9, 2012, Line 14. Plaintiff Richard Hylen'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Continued from December 15, 2011 at the parties' request to provide them an opportunity to resolve their differences. The court reiterates its prior tentative ruling: Both parties are entitled to receive a gross payment of $7240.70 every four weeks less credit card charges attributable to them. If the parties cannot agree on whether this amount is employee compensation, a distribution of or a draw against profits, withdrawal of capital or something else, subject to a final characterization at trial, the amounts will be treated as a draw against profits. Hylen is entitled to recover past amounts since the date Ryder listed himself as an employee with Paychex. If, however, Ryder does not return to and remain at work full-time (excepting vacations and short illnesses) by January 1, 2012 the payments cease as of January 1, 2012 or such later date that Ryder ceases to work full-time. Hylen has full authority to choose Satel's payroll provider and is Satel's representative for that provider. The court finds that the parties had an agreement to pay themselves while each were working full-time for Satel and Hylen unilaterally and without authority breached that agreement to the detriment of Ryder. The court further finds that, due to Ryder's recent leave of absence and his listing himself as an employee with Paychex without disclosing this to Hylen, Hylen should be assigned the responsibility to work with Satel's payroll provider. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 9, 2012, Line 21. Defendant Khong Tu'S Motion To Strike Complaint. Granted. Defendant satisfied his burden of showing that all causes of action asserted by plaintiff arise from protected conduct and defendant failed to satisfy his burden that any of his claim have merit.
